narrative:
Today, we recover from the shock to the system weather of yesterday. We went from June to January in one day and today, we get back to March. It is a little breezy but otherwise pleasant. Saturday is even nicer with milder temperatures. Mild temperatures continue Sunday but light rain, drizzle and fog develop in the afternoon and continue overnight. Monday brings another shock to the system with much colder air, strong winds and snow showers and flurries taking over after morning rain.

The "Mix and Match" Special Offer continues on all the Snowman O'Mometer Pint Glass and Mug (or you can long for a 5 Golfball Day with that pint glass as well). Buy one, and get another drinkware item at 40% off.

Recovering

Last Updated Fri March 13 at 10:32AM by Wayne Presnell

Today, we recover from the shock to the system weather of yesterday. We went from June to January in one day and today, we get back to March. It is a little breezy but otherwise pleasant. Saturday is even nicer with milder temperatures. Mild temperatures continue Sunday but light rain, drizzle and fog develop in the afternoon and continue overnight. Monday brings another shock to the system with much colder air, strong winds and snow showers and flurries taking over after morning rain.

The "Mix and Match" Special Offer continues on all the Snowman O'Mometer Pint Glass and Mug (or you can long for a 5 Golfball Day with that pint glass as well). Buy one, and get another drinkware item at 40% off.

Forecast Discussion

High pressure to the east gives us a nice day today and an even nicer day tomorrow. A cold front will stall over NC Saturday but it will have little impact on our weather.

The stalled front backdoors (moves in from the northeast) into western NC on Sunday. The low level flow turns southeasterly. This sets up a scenario for low clouds, fog and drizzle to develop Sunday afternoon. Spotty light rain develops as well and all of this continues into Sunday night.

A strong cold front will move through Monday morning. This system will provide another shock to the system like yesterday's front did. However, the temperatures ahead of the front are not as warm and the temperatures behind the front are much colder. Rain (maybe some thunderstorms) changes to snow before ending by afternoon and snow showers and flurries continue into the evening. Winds will be very strong and gusty Monday into Tuesday afternoon. Winds die down Tuesday night and Wednesday as high pressure is centered overhead.

Low temperatures fall into the teens Tuesday and Wednesday mornings. Temperatures recover Wednesday afternoon and even moreso Thursday as high pressure is overhead Wednesday and slides to the east Thursday.

After Monday's precipitation, we are likely dry until the following weekend.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.

Latest Insights
It is, after all, March....

March Madness rules supreme! After record high temperatures on Wednesday, temperatures at noon today are around 40 degrees colder vs. this time on Wednesday.

Oh, and yeah, it snowed in the mountains this morning!

Expect more wild swings this weekend and early next week.

Mar 12, 2026
Monday Message for March 9, 2026

It's March Madness, weather-style, around western North Carolina. We discuss the ups-and-downs of March weather in this week's edition.

Mar 09, 2026
Spring Fever and near-record temperatures

Warm weather is here, at least until the middle of next week. We will be flirting with daily records at a number of locations during this time period.

On Friday, high temperatures will be in record territory for many locations.

On this map, a '1' denotes a forecast high temperature that would surpass a high temperature record for the 6th of March.
